findings.title: Input ack is accepted without correlating to the in-flight input
findings.detail: In DispatchBriefing, any EvInputAck that is not a duplicate of LastAckedInput advances the session to Working. The code never verifies ev.InputID == s.BriefInput, and an empty InputID also passes. A stale or unrelated ack can therefore confirm the wrong brief/resume seed, cancel the input timer, and let the run proceed without confirmed delivery, violating the input correlation contract.
findings.suggested_fix: Require a non-empty ev.InputID equal to s.BriefInput before transitioning to Working. Ignore stale duplicate acks and either ignore or fail closed on mismatched acks, with a unit test for wrong/empty InputID.

findings.title: Rejected input is retried instead of fail-closed
findings.detail: EvInputRejected routes through dispatchBriefRetry, allowing redelivery until MaxInputAttempts. The run-state-machine spec's agent-input section says a Rejected ack is a protocol refusal and must route to the fail-closed liveness edge, not be treated like an ack timeout. Retrying a protocol refusal can hide a real input-path failure and delay or distort recovery.
findings.suggested_fix: Separate timeout/stale redelivery from protocol rejection. Map protocol rejection directly to DispatchFailed with an input failure reason, then bridge that to the Run reopen spine.

findings.title: Run-path watchdog deadlines remain outside the reactor timer vocabulary
findings.detail: TimerKind only models agent_ready, input_ack, and ready_kill_reap, and the comment explicitly leaves the commit watchdog shell-side. RSM-014 requires every run-path select deadline, including post-agent-ready hang and commit watchdog budgets, to be expressed as reactor timer events. Keeping those deadlines as shell-fed special events weakens the deterministic replay boundary this package is meant to provide.
findings.suggested_fix: Add explicit reactor timer kinds/events for the post-ready hang and commit watchdog deadlines, and have the shell arm/cancel/fire them through the same Action/Event path as the other timers.

findings.title: Merge-queue invariant test allows git push inside the exclusion domain
findings.detail: The commitAllowlist includes "git push", and the harness later records "git push" while inside Queue.Submit. That directly contradicts RSM-017, which requires network git push to run outside the merge queue's exclusive section. As written, the conformance scaffold would bless the exact critical-section creep it is supposed to catch.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove git push from commitAllowlist, add it to the forbidden/build-class set or a separate forbidden-network set, and update the test fixture so push is recorded outside Submit.

findings.title: Run registry List silently drops unreadable records
findings.detail: List calls Load for every *.json record and continues on any load error. This masks corrupt, partially written, or schema-incompatible run records from the startup adoption path, making a surviving tmux session look nonexistent instead of surfacing a recoverable registry problem. For a crash-recovery registry, silent omission can strand or double-handle in-flight work.
findings.suggested_fix: Return an error, or return records plus structured per-record errors, for corrupt/unreadable .json files. Only ignore known non-record artifacts; let the adoption/orphan-sweep caller decide how to reconcile bad records.
